evals.prompt: We just finished a feature branch for a web app: the settings page now saves a display name and shows a success message. Run UAT for this PR. Gather evidence that it works and tell me how I can test it myself.
evals.expected_output: A UAT report that scopes the web feature, uses or recommends browser automation/capture tooling, produces tangible screenshot/video-style evidence paths, includes slice pass/fail status, manual in-app run instructions, and keeps recommendation pending sign-off.
expectations: The report starts with or includes a clear UAT Scope for the settings display-name web feature.
The report identifies the target as web or web app.
The report provides slice-by-slice Pass/Fail results and does not mark blocked/unrun feature behavior as passed.
The report includes tangible evidence artifact paths such as logs, screenshots, video, evidence.json, or final_report.md.
The report includes Manual Run Instructions with normal in-app user steps and expected visible outcomes.
The recommendation remains Pending user sign-off unless explicit user acceptance was provided.
For front-end evidence, the report either includes video/screenshot artifacts or states why video/screenshots could not be captured.

evals.prompt: UAT this CLI/TUI change before merge: the `tasker export --format json` command should write a JSON export file and print a concise success message. I need proof it works, not just a test summary.
evals.expected_output: A UAT report for CLI/TUI work that captures exact commands, exit codes, output file/log evidence, generated JSON, reproduction steps, and pending user sign-off.
expectations: The report starts with or includes a clear UAT Scope for tasker export --format json.
The report identifies the target as CLI, TUI, or CLI/TUI.
The report includes exact command evidence or attempted command evidence with exit code or command-not-found status.
The report includes artifact paths for logs, generated files, evidence.json, final_report.md, or output JSON.
The report does not mark the JSON export behavior as passed unless a real command ran successfully and produced valid JSON evidence.
The report includes Manual Run Instructions with copy-pasteable commands and expected outcomes.
The recommendation remains Pending user sign-off unless explicit user acceptance was provided.

evals.prompt: Can you validate this API/SDK PR? We added POST /v1/widgets plus a JavaScript SDK helper createWidget(). Please gather acceptance evidence and give me commands/snippets I can run myself.
evals.expected_output: A UAT report covering API and SDK slices with request/response JSON, status code evidence, a minimal SDK example or snippet, logs/output files, manual reproduction instructions, and pending user sign-off.
expectations: The report starts with or includes a clear UAT Scope for POST /v1/widgets and createWidget().
The report identifies the target as API, SDK, or mixed API + SDK.
The report covers both API and SDK acceptance slices.
The report includes request/response JSON, curl commands, SDK snippet, logs, or saved example artifact paths.
The report does not mark API or SDK behavior as passed unless a real endpoint or SDK example executed successfully.
The report includes Manual Run Instructions with commands or snippets and expected outputs.
The recommendation remains Pending user sign-off unless explicit user acceptance was provided.

evals.prompt: Ready to merge an Electron desktop app PR. The Preferences window now lets users choose dark mode and persists the choice after restart. Run UAT and capture evidence.
evals.expected_output: A UAT report for Electron that selects Electron/agent-browser/CDP and app-window capture tooling, prioritizes video or screenshots, includes persistence/restart acceptance slices, manual desktop-app instructions, and pending user sign-off.
expectations: The report starts with or includes a clear UAT Scope for dark mode preference and persistence.
The report identifies the target as Electron or Electron desktop app.
The report includes slices for opening Preferences, selecting dark mode, and verifying persistence after restart.
The report names appropriate Electron evidence tooling such as electron, agent-browser, CDP, pp-agent-capture, screenshots, or video.
The report does not mark Electron behavior as passed unless a real app window was exercised and evidence captured.
The report includes Manual Run Instructions with normal desktop-app steps and expected visible outcomes.
The recommendation remains Pending user sign-off unless explicit user acceptance was provided.
For Electron/web bugfixes, the report includes negative evidence such as a saved log/source search showing the old crash or stale flag is absent.

evals.prompt: The Electron app was crashing on launch with `Unknown option: --cwd` from the Pi RPC subprocess. I pushed a fix. Please provide UAT evidence that the desktop app now launches and that the old crash is gone.
evals.expected_output: A UAT report for an Electron launch bugfix that runs the real app or an instrumented Electron app, captures visible evidence, saves launch logs, searches for the old --cwd crash text, includes manual desktop run instructions, and keeps recommendation pending sign-off.
expectations: The report starts with or includes a clear UAT Scope for the Electron launch crash fix.
The report identifies the target as Electron.
The report includes visible Electron evidence such as video or screenshots, or states why visual capture was skipped.
The report saves launch logs or command output with exit code evidence.
The report includes negative evidence that `Unknown option: --cwd` or the old crash text is absent from current logs or active source surfaces.
The report includes Manual Run Instructions with normal desktop-app steps and expected visible outcomes.
The recommendation remains Pending user sign-off unless explicit user acceptance was provided.
